data.title: Santiment: The current average ETH transfer fee is only $0.41, and user return is expected to drive medium- and long-term price increases
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Santiment published an article on X stating that currently, the average fee for Ethereum transfers is only $0.41, while the highest point in the past two years was $15.21. When Ethereum transaction fees are low, it usually means that the network is not too crowded. When users do not have to pay high fees when transferring ETH or tokens, this is usually a good sign for medium- and long-term price prospects. Traders believe that these low fee levels usually appear when prices are seen as "low" or "bearish." These reduced costs make it easier for new buyers to enter the market.
On the other hand, when fees are high, it usually means that there is a lot of activity on the network. This usually happens when ETH prices rise rapidly as more and more people rush to trade or use Ethereum-based applications. Just as extremely high fees sometimes discourage traders or lead to short-term adjustments, extremely low fees (like we are seeing now) usually incentivize users to return and increase the utility of the network to a prosperous level.

data.title: SEC Seeks Public Comments on Approving Grayscale and Bitwise Ethereum ETF Options Trading
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News The U.S. SEC is seeking public comments on whether to allow Cboe Exchange to list and trade options on Grayscale Ethereum Trust ETF, Grayscale Ethereum Mini Trust ETF and Bitwise Ethereum ETF.
Comments should be submitted within 21 days of publication in the Federal Register. The SEC has not yet approved options trading for spot Ethereum ETFs. The SEC approved several spot Ethereum ETFs in May 2024, and companies have been scrambling to obtain options trading licenses since then.
The SEC also approved spot Bitcoin ETFs a year ago and allowed options trading for these products later in 2024. (The Block)

data.title: Trump Signs Executive Order To Increase Oversight Of Federal Agencies
data.description: According to Odaily, U.S. President Donald Trump signed an executive order on February 18, aiming to enhance oversight of several independent federal regulatory agencies, including the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC), the Federal Trade Commission, and the Federal Communications Commission. The order mandates these agencies to submit draft regulations for White House review and consult with the Trump administration on their priorities and strategic plans. While independent federal agencies typically follow White House directives, they are not required to do so by their mandates. This executive order may lead to legal challenges from these regulatory bodies.

data.title: Bank Of New York Mellon Holds Over $13 Million In Bitcoin ETFs
data.description: According to PANews, recent filings with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission reveal that Bank of New York Mellon held over $13 million in Bitcoin ETFs by the end of the fourth quarter. The holdings include 115,108 shares of the WisdomTree Bitcoin Fund (BTCW), valued at approximately $11.87 million, and 25,309 shares of the BlackRock iShares Bitcoin Trust (IBIT), valued at around $1.4 million.

data.title: Scam Sniffer: Scammers use fake vulnerability claims to target Jupiter users in phishing campaign
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to Scam Sniffer, there is currently an active phishing campaign targeting Jupiter users. The false "Jupiter Exchange vulnerability" statement is being spread through advertisements, and scammers urge users to paste malicious code into the browser console, which will lead to wallet theft. Please be vigilant and beware of risks.

data.title: Phishing Alert Targets Jupiter Users With Fake Exchange Vulnerability
data.description: According to Odaily, Scam Sniffer has detected an active phishing campaign targeting Jupiter users. Fraudsters are spreading false claims of a 'Jupiter Exchange vulnerability' through advertisements, urging users to paste malicious code into their browser consoles. This action can lead to wallet theft. Users are advised to remain vigilant and be cautious of potential risks.

data.title: Trump signs executive order to tighten restrictions on independent federal regulators such as the SEC
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: US President Trump signed an executive order on February 18, local time, attempting to strengthen constraints on a number of independent federal regulatory agencies such as the US Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC), the Federal Trade Commission, and the Federal Communications Commission. The executive order requires these agencies to submit draft regulations to the White House for review before issuing them, and to consult with the Trump administration on their priorities and strategic plans. Independent federal agencies usually follow the White House's instructions, but their duties do not require this. This executive order may trigger legal challenges from these regulatory agencies. (Jinshi)

data.title: Blockchain Platform DigitFT to Tokenize $6.3 Billion Invesco Private Credit Fund
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Singapore blockchain platform DigitFT will offer a tokenized version of the $6.3 billion Invesco private credit fund.
According to a statement from DigitFT on Wednesday, institutional investors can use U.S. dollars or stablecoins USDC and USDT to purchase tokenized shares of the underlying Invesco fund. DigitFT CEO Henry Zhang said that the Invesco fund invests in the company's senior secured loans and has an annual net yield of 4.5% since its inception in 2006.
According to data provider rwa.xyz, private credit accounts for about 80% of tokenized RWA to date. Invesco, which has an asset management scale of about $1.9 trillion, said in a report last November that by 2030, the assets of tokenized funds could swell to $600 billion, accounting for about 1% of the total assets managed by mutual funds and exchange-traded funds.
Zhang said that DigiFT's goal is to eventually provide instant settlement for the tokenized version of the Invesco fund, while the settlement time for the underlying instrument takes up to five days. (Bloomberg)

data.title: Howard Lutnick Confirmed As U.S. Commerce Secretary
data.description: According to BlockBeats, Howard Lutnick, a proponent of cryptocurrency, has been confirmed by the U.S. Senate to serve as the United States Secretary of Commerce. Lutnick is known to hold a substantial investment in Bitcoin, valued at several hundred million dollars.

data.title: Nvidia-backed robotics AI startup Field AI is raising hundreds of millions of dollars at a $2 billion valuation
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: According to people familiar with the matter, robotics AI startup Field AI is currently in talks to raise hundreds of millions of dollars at a valuation of $2 billion. This will quadruple the company's valuation, which was valued at $500 million by investors including Nvidia last summer. (The Information)
